description: Having an acidic taste like lemon or vinegar; tart and sharp. 'Tuko' names the quality of sourness—the taste that puckers, that signals fermentation or unripeness.
sound_symbolism: The word puckers. 'Tu' begins with tongue pressed forward, tartness forming. Then 'ko' catches with sharp clarity, sourness confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'tuko tiroa' (sour fruit). Related to 'puko' (bitter) but describes acidic tang rather than sharp unpleasantness.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sour tastes signaled preservation—fermented foods carried beneficial sourness that indicated safe transformation.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sour experiences test patience—accepting discomfort without aversion, tasting fully what arises.
